title: Practitioner · provider (core)
notes:
Branch dimension: how the Practitioner__provider edge fills the OMOP provider row from a single Practitioner. Grounded in mapspec/views/Practitioner__provider.view.json (stage 1) + mapspec/etl/Practitioner__provider.sql (stage 2), and copied from the real cdm_ours_fhir.provider row for Practitioner e17c3635-2ea1-316f-bbfc-5b99857bcd63 (provider_name 'Ritchie586, Carter549', npi 9999990192, gender male → 8507/8507).

Column-by-column mechanics (stage 2):
  provider_id              = hashtextextended(Practitioner.id, 0)::bigint  — ALWAYS the Practitioner.id UUID, never NPI. This equals referenceToId(id), so it is asserted via 'ref:prac-1'. (Verified live: hashtextextended('e17c3635-…',0) = referenceToId('e17c3635-…') = -5977988087127504051.)
  provider_name            = coalesce(family,'') || ', ' || coalesce(given,'')  — note family/surname comes FIRST, then ', ', then given. The view extracts family via name.first().family and given via name.first().given.first(). Because both sides are coalesced to '', provider_name is ALWAYS non-null — with no name at all it is the literal ', '.
  npi                      = Practitioner.identifier.where(system='http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/us-npi').value.first()  — standalone traceability column; NULL when no us-npi identifier.
  dea                      = NULL (constant, never populated)
  specialty_concept_id     = 38004446 (CONSTANT — ETL-Synthea hardcodes 'Family Practice'; this concept_id resolves to 'General Practice' in the loaded Athena bundle). Practitioner ALONE carries no specialty — specialty would come from PractitionerRole, which this edge does not consume — so this is a fixed constant on every row, never derived.
  care_site_id             = NULL (constant)
  year_of_birth            = NULL (constant — Practitioner has no birthDate in Synthea)
  gender_concept_id        = CASE lower(gender): male→8507, female→8532, other→8521, unknown→8551, ELSE 0
  provider_source_value    = Practitioner.id UUID (traceability anchor; NPI lives in the npi column, not here)
  specialty_source_value   = NULL (constant)
  specialty_source_concept_id = 38004446 (CONSTANT, same as specialty_concept_id)
  gender_source_value      = Practitioner.gender verbatim (NULL when absent)
  gender_source_concept_id = CASE lower(gender): male→8507, female→8532, ELSE 0  — note this CASE ONLY handles male/female; 'other'/'unknown'/absent all yield 0, so gender_concept_id and gender_source_concept_id DIVERGE for other/unknown.

Concept_ids verified in vocab.concept: 8507 MALE, 8532 FEMALE, 8521 OTHER, 8551 UNKNOWN, 38004446 'General Practice'.

Every variant emits exactly ONE provider row and nothing else.

cases.desc: full practitioner: name + NPI + gender male → provider_name 'family, given', npi, gender_concept_id 8507 AND gender_source_concept_id 8507; specialty hardcoded 38004446 (copied from real cdm_ours_fhir.provider)
gender 'other' → gender_concept_id 8521 BUT gender_source_concept_id 0 (source CASE only handles male/female, so the two gender concept columns DIVERGE)
no gender → gender_concept_id 0, gender_source_value NULL, gender_source_concept_id 0 (variant b: missing gender)
no NPI identifier and no name → npi NULL, provider_name is the literal ', ' (both family and given coalesce to ''); gender male still 8507
